message:
test(conformance): parity packet fixtures for string-method + head/tail edge cases

Adds 28 new pandas-parity conformance packets covering edge cases that
were previously uncovered or exercised only via ad-hoc unit tests. All
fixtures run in `strict` mode so any deviation from pandas fails.

DataFrame head/tail (165/166)
- FP-P2D-165 / FP-P2D-166: oversized `n` argument returning the whole
  frame unchanged, pinning the DataFrame metadata + row-identity
  behavior.

Series.str coverage
- 170 upper, 171 capitalize: empty-string + leading-symbol + Unicode
  inputs.
- 174 startswith / 175 endswith: empty-pattern behavior (pandas returns
  True for every row).
- 197 swapcase: symbol + greek-letter runs exercising the context-
  sensitive run path added in the preceding commit; Unicode-expansion
  case for sharp s and ligature decompositions.
- 203 isdigit / 209 isnumeric / 224 isdecimal: mixed-sign inputs,
  Unicode inputs that are numeric-looking but not digit/decimal, ASCII
  boundary cases.
- 207 islower / 208 isupper: Unicode-symbol-only strings (pandas returns
  False because there is no cased character).
- 220 removeprefix / 221 removesuffix: Unicode exact-match-only plus
  single-character suffix exact-only cases.
- 225 istitle: punctuation + numeric word-boundary cases and Unicode
  word-boundary cases.
- 226 casefold: final sigma (σ/ς) collapse plus sharp-s / Unicode
  expansion cases that motivated the switch to `unicode-casefold` in
  the preceding commit.

All fixtures follow the established strict-mode packet format
(`packet_id`, `case_id`, `mode`, `operation`, `left`, `right`) so they
are picked up by the fp-conformance harness without any runner changes.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,43 @@
+{
+  "packet_id": "FP-P2D-207",
+  "case_id": "series_str_islower_unicode_symbols_strict",
+  "mode": "strict",
+  "operation": "series_str_islower",
+  "left": {
+    "name": "text",
+    "index": [
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 4 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 4 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 8 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 9 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 11 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 11 }
+    ],
+    "values": [
+      { "kind": "str", "value": "éclair" },
+      { "kind": "str", "value": "ß" },
+      { "kind": "str", "value": "123" },
+      { "kind": "str", "value": "--" },
+      { "kind": "str", "value": "δ123!" },
+      { "kind": "str", "value": "δΣ" }
+    ]
+  },
+  "expected_series": {
+    "index": [
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 4 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 4 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 8 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 9 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 11 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 11 }
+    ],
+    "values": [
+      { "kind": "bool", "value": true },
+      { "kind": "bool", "value": true },
+      { "kind": "bool", "value": false },
+      { "kind": "bool", "value": false },
+      { "kind": "bool", "value": true },
+      { "kind": "bool", "value": false }
+    ]
+  }
+}

+    ],
+    "values": [

+      { "kind": "bool", "value": false },

+    ]
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,43 @@
+{

+  "case_id": "series_str_istitle_punctuation_numeric_boundaries_strict",
+  "mode": "strict",
+  "operation": "series_str_istitle",
+  "left": {

+    "index": [

+}
